skill.name: ai-research-reproduction
skill.description: Rigor Reproduce compatible skill slug for README-first deep learning repository reproduction. Use when the user wants an end-to-end, minimal-trustworthy flow that reads the repository first, selects the smallest documented inference or evaluation target, coordinates intake, setup, trusted execution, optional trusted training, optional repository analysis, and optional paper-gap resolution, enforces conservative patch rules, records evidence assumptions deviations and human decision points, and writes the standardized `repro_outputs/` bundle. Do not use for paper summary, generic environment setup, isolated repo scanning, standalone command execution, silent protocol changes, score chasing, or broad research assistance outside repository-grounded reproduction.
skill.summary: Rigor Reproduce compatible skill slug for README-first deep learning repository reproduction. Use wh...
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 178 patterns with a risk score of 100/100, but the vast majority are false positives. Backtick patterns are markdown formatting in template and reference docs, not code execution. The 'weak crypto' hits are textual references to hash documentation, not active cryptography. The script orchestrate_repro.py uses subprocess.run with shlex.split and proper timeouts to run documented README commands as part of the reproduction flow. No data exfiltration, no obfuscated payloads, no prompt injection attempts were found. The skill is a legitimate research reproduction orchestrator with expected external command and filesystem access.

medium_findings.title: External command execution via subprocess.run with shlex.split
medium_findings.description: The orchestrate_repro.py script uses subprocess.run with shlex.split(posix=False) to execute documented commands extracted from a target repository's README. Commands run with a configurable timeout and are derived from parsed README content. While there is no direct user-input concatenation into a shell string, the skill inherently executes arbitrary documented commands from third-party repos. This is by design for repository reproduction but warrants operator awareness.

medium_findings.title: Wide attack surface from broad backtick pattern matches in documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 150+ 'backtick execution' patterns across template and reference markdown files. Evaluation confirms these are markdown inline-code formatting (e.g., `python train.py`), not executable code. However, the volume reflects a skill that processes and references many shell command examples that an agent will read and potentially act upon.

low_findings.title: Temporary file creation for context JSON
low_findings.description: orchestrate_repro.py uses tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile to pass context JSON to sub-scripts. File is unlinked in a finally block. Standard, low-risk pattern.

low_findings.title: System information detection for environment documentation
low_findings.description: References to detecting system info (Python version, platform) for environment documentation purposes. No exfiltration; data is written to local repro_outputs/.

content.user_title: Reproduce AI research repositories with auditable evidence
content.value_statement: Reproducing deep learning papers is slow and error-prone because commands, datasets, and assumptions are scattered across READMEs. This skill reads the repository first, selects the smallest documented target, and writes a standardized repro_outputs/ bundle with evidence, deviations, and human decision points.
actual_capabilities: Reads a target AI repository's README and extracts documented commands and candidate targets before any execution
Selects the smallest trustworthy reproduction target (inference, evaluation, training smoke, then full training only on confirmation)
Writes a standardized repro_outputs/ bundle with SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, status.json, and optional PATCHES.md
Records assumptions, deviations, and human decision points to keep reproduction auditable
Enforces a conservative patch policy that prefers CLI flags and environment fixes over silent code changes
Coordinates intake, setup, execution, training verification, analysis, and paper-gap resolution across sub-skills
limitations: Designed only for README-grounded AI code repositories, not paper summaries or open-ended research design
Does not silently change model architecture, loss functions, metrics, or core training logic
Full training runs require explicit user confirmation and are not auto-launched
Quality of reproduction depends on the target repository's README clarity and completeness

use_cases.title: Reproduce a published deep learning paper's inference
use_cases.description: A researcher wants to verify a paper's reported numbers by running the documented evaluation script on the released checkpoint.

use_cases.title: Audit a forked repository's reproduction quality
use_cases.description: A research engineer needs to assess whether a fork faithfully reproduces the original paper and where deviations occurred.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap a new repository's reproduction workflow
use_cases.description: A lab lead wants a consistent, auditable workflow for new students to reproduce papers without silent protocol changes.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new reproduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-research-reproduction skill on the repository at <PATH>. Read the README first, select the smallest documented target, and produce a repro_outputs/ bundle.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: first-time use with a new repo

prompt_templates.title: Reproduce inference only
prompt_templates.prompt: Reproduce only the documented inference target for this repository. Do not run training. Record the command, stdout/stderr, and any deviations in repro_outputs/.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: restrict to a single phase

prompt_templates.title: Reproduce evaluation with custom checkpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Reproduce the documented evaluation using the checkpoint at <CKPT_PATH>. Treat the README as the primary intent, and record any conflicts between README and code in LOG.md.

prompt_templates.title: Full reproduction with paper-gap resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: Reproduce the documented training target end-to-end. If the README and paper conflict, pause for human review and use paper-context-resolver only for the narrow reproduction-critical gap. Write all evidence to repro_outputs/.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: full training with gap resolution

output_examples.input: User asks to reproduce inference in /workspace/repo.
output: Selected target: documented inference (smallest trustworthy target).
Wrote SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, status.json under repro_outputs/.
No deviations detected. No patches applied.

output_examples.input: User asks to reproduce training, but README only documents evaluation.
output: Selected target: documented evaluation (smaller than full training).
Main blocker: README does not document a training command; full training requires explicit user confirmation.
Wrote repro_outputs/ with status=blocked and a human decision point in LOG.md.
Awaiting user confirmation before considering training kickoff.

best_practices: Always start by reading the README and treat it as the primary reproduction intent before scanning other files.
Prefer the smallest trustworthy target: inference before evaluation, evaluation before training smoke, full training only on confirmation.
Keep patches conservative and auditable: prefer CLI flags, env vars, and dependency fixes over silent code changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for paper summaries, generic environment setup, or isolated repo scanning outside reproduction.
Do not silently change model architecture, loss functions, metrics, or training logic to make a run succeed.
Do not launch full training runs without explicit user confirmation and a recorded decision point.

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: It guides an agent to reproduce an AI repository in a minimal, trustworthy way: read the README, pick the smallest documented target, run it with evidence, and write a standardized repro_outputs/ bundle.

faq.question: Can it run full training automatically?
faq.answer: No. Full training requires explicit user confirmation. The skill will pause and record a human decision point before any full training kickoff.

faq.question: Does it modify the target repository?
faq.answer: It prefers no edits. If patches are necessary, they are conservative (CLI flags, env vars, dependency fixes) and recorded in PATCHES.md with README-fidelity impact.

faq.question: Is it safe to run on third-party repositories?
faq.answer: The skill executes documented commands from the target repository's README with a timeout and no shell-string concatenation. Operators should still review the README commands before running, as with any reproduction task.

faq.question: What output files does it produce?
faq.answer: It writes SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, status.json, and optionally PATCHES.md under repro_outputs/ in the working directory.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, as it is a documentation and orchestration skill rather than a tool-specific plugin.
